Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Helleboraster multicaulis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant can be found in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and to promote relaxation.
